modes.name: Pyrolysis
modes.description: Heats the sample in stages. Volatiles evaporate at different temperature thresholds — higher temperatures release more tightly-bound molecules.
modes.hint: Best for organics, volatiles, and water-bound compounds. Use mouse Y to control oven temperature.

modes.name: Wet Chemistry
modes.description: Dissolves a portion of the sample in chemical reagent, then runs the solution through the mass spectrometer. Requires subsurface ice as solvent base.
modes.hint: Best for mineral ID and elemental ratios. Drag sliders to balance reagent mix. Requires 1 ice per run.

modes.name: Isotope Analysis
modes.description: Fires a laser through released gases, measuring absorption at specific wavelengths. Identifies isotope ratios that fingerprint biological vs geological origin.
modes.hint: Best on samples with interesting pyrolysis/wet-chem results. Tune laser with mouse X — lock onto peaks, avoid artifacts.

discoveries.name: Volcanic glass
discoveries.description: Amorphous silicate formed during rapid lava cooling. Common on volcanic plains.

discoveries.name: Iron(III) oxide
discoveries.description: Fe₂O₃ — the compound that makes Mars red. Abundant everywhere.

discoveries.name: Forsterite
discoveries.description: Mg₂SiO₄ crystal. Deep mantle mineral that reached the surface through volcanic uplift.

discoveries.name: Calcium sulfate
discoveries.description: Anhydrite/gypsum. Precipitated from ancient evaporating water bodies.

discoveries.name: Montmorillonite clay
discoveries.description: Smectite clay formed by chemical weathering in the presence of water.

discoveries.name: Kamacite
discoveries.description: Iron-nickel alloy. The backbone of iron meteorites. Not from Mars.

discoveries.name: Adsorbed water
discoveries.description: Trace water molecules clinging to mineral surfaces. Mars isn't completely dry.

discoveries.name: Geological carbon
discoveries.description: Standard C12/C13 ratio. Volcanic outgassing carbon — no biological origin.

discoveries.name: Manganese oxide
discoveries.description: MnO₂ — on Earth, often produced by microbial activity. On Mars... unclear.

discoveries.name: Serpentinization evidence
discoveries.description: Olivine + water → serpentine + hydrogen. The hydrogen could feed microbial life.

discoveries.name: Chlorinated compounds
discoveries.description: Chloromethane and dichloromethane. Could be contamination. Could be Martian. Controversial.

discoveries.name: Organic volatiles
discoveries.description: Low-temperature carbon compounds released during gentle heating. The first hint.

discoveries.name: Platinum group metals
discoveries.description: Iridium, platinum, osmium. Concentrated in meteorites.

discoveries.name: Ancient water signature
discoveries.description: D/H ratio indicates this rock interacted with liquid water billions of years ago.

discoveries.name: Phyllosilicate
discoveries.description: Layered clay mineral — forms only in sustained liquid water. Habitable environment indicator.

discoveries.name: Perchlorate chemistry
discoveries.description: ClO₄⁻ salts. Toxic to humans but fascinating chemistry.

discoveries.name: Thiophenic compounds
discoveries.description: Sulfur-bearing organics. Found by the real Curiosity at Gale Crater. Consistent with ancient biology.

discoveries.name: Nickel anomaly
discoveries.description: Nickel concentration far above Martian background. Meteoritic input to the sediment?

discoveries.name: Ancient brine chemistry
discoveries.description: Halite and perchlorate ratios that reconstruct the salinity of a vanished Martian lake.

discoveries.name: Presolar grains
discoveries.description: Mineral grains older than the solar system. Formed in a dying star before our Sun existed.

discoveries.name: Biological sulfur ratio
discoveries.description: S32/S34 ratio showing mass-independent fractionation — a potential biosignature.

discoveries.name: Biosignature candidate
discoveries.description: C12-enriched carbon. Biology preferentially uses lighter isotopes. This is the signal.

discoveries.name: Deep mantle signature
discoveries.description: Noble gas ratios from deep within Mars. A window into the planet's interior.

discoveries.name: Widmanstätten pattern
discoveries.description: Nickel-iron crystal structure that takes millions of years to form. This rock is ancient.

discoveries.name: Aromatic organics
discoveries.description: Benzene, toluene, and chlorobenzene. Complex carbon chemistry in ancient lakebed sediment. The headline discovery.

discoveries.name: Confirmed biosignature
discoveries.description: C12/C13 ratio combined with organic compounds produces a statistically significant biological signature. This is the one.

discoveries.name: Lipid biomarker
discoveries.description: Long-chain fatty acids preserved in clay matrix. On Earth, these are always biological in origin.

discoveries.name: Extraterrestrial organic
discoveries.description: Amino acids and nucleobases embedded in the meteorite matrix. Life's building blocks, delivered from space.

discoveries.name: Extrasolar isotope
discoveries.description: Isotope ratios that don't match any known solar system source. This grain came from another star.

discoveries.name: Chlorinated organic
discoveries.description: Complex chlorinated hydrocarbon in evaporite deposit. Not contamination. Martian.

discoveries.name: Hydrothermal vent signature
discoveries.description: Helium-3 and neon isotope ratios characteristic of deep mantle hydrothermal output. This rock sat on the floor of a Martian hydrothermal vent.

discoveries.name: Iron isotope fractionation
discoveries.description: Fe⁵⁶/Fe⁵⁴ ratio reveals whether this iron was processed by water or magma. Aqueous fractionation detected.

discoveries.name: Sulfur volatilization profile
discoveries.description: Temperature-staged sulfur release. SO₂ at 400°C, then H₂S at 600°C — classic evaporite signature.

discoveries.name: Nickel provenance
discoveries.description: Ni⁶⁰/Ni⁵⁸ ratio confirms meteoritic origin. This nickel came from the core of a differentiated asteroid.

discoveries.name: Manganese redox state
discoveries.description: Dissolved Mn shows +4 oxidation state. On Earth this requires either oxygen or microbial mediation.

discoveries.name: Silicon polymorph ID
discoveries.description: Thermal expansion signature identifies cristobalite — a high-temperature SiO₂ polymorph from volcanic activity.

discoveries.name: Magnesium carbonate
discoveries.description: MgCO₃ dissolved from the trace sample. Magnesite forms in the presence of CO₂-saturated water.

discoveries.name: Calcium isotope anomaly
discoveries.description: Ca⁴⁴/Ca⁴⁰ excess. This calcium was fractionated in an ancient hydrothermal system.

discoveries.name: Sodium chloride crystal
discoveries.description: NaCl precipitated from dissolution. Halite — the simplest evidence that briny water once existed here.

discoveries.name: Titanium oxide phase
discoveries.description: Anatase-to-rutile transition at 600°C. The phase change fingerprints volcanic temperature history.

discoveries.name: Aluminum phyllosilicate
discoveries.description: Kaolinite detected in dissolution — aluminum clay that forms only through prolonged water contact.

discoveries.name: Biological iron reduction
discoveries.description: Fe⁵⁶ depletion pattern consistent with dissimilatory iron reduction — a metabolism used by anaerobic microbes on Earth.

discoveries.name: Biogenic sulfur fractionation
discoveries.description: Mass-independent S³³ anomaly in trace sulfur. On Earth, this signature is exclusively biological.

discoveries.name: Basalt glass fiber draw
discoveries.description: High-temperature pyrolysis melts volcanic glass into a drawable melt. Thin silicate fibers pulled from the cooling strand — lightweight structural reinforcement.

discoveries.name: Iron electrodeposition
discoveries.description: Dissolved hematite in acid solution, then electroplated onto a cathode wire. Pure iron filament deposits atom by atom.

discoveries.name: Meteoritic cold-working
discoveries.description: Acid-etching isolates high-purity kamacite-taenite phases. The nickel-iron is cold-worked into a dense structural billet.

discoveries.name: Silicate extraction
discoveries.description: Basic thermal decomposition releases silicate melt. Low-yield fiber draw from general volcanic minerals.

discoveries.name: Perchlorate extraction
discoveries.description: Dissolving evaporite deposits and selectively precipitating perchlorate salts. Concentrated oxidizer — handle with care.

discoveries.name: Sulfuric acid distillation
discoveries.description: Thermal decomposition of calcium sulfate releases SO₃ gas. Captured and hydrated to produce sulfuric acid. The smell is unforgettable.

discoveries.name: Magnesium reduction
discoveries.description: Acid dissolution of forsterite crystals, then precipitation of magnesium salts from solution. Silvery powder, dangerously reactive in oxygen.

discoveries.name: Olivine acid leach
discoveries.description: Simple acid dissolution yielding low-purity magnesium and silica residue. Not elegant, but it works.

discoveries.name: Volatile polymerization
discoveries.description: Capturing organic volatiles at precisely controlled temperatures and catalyzing chain polymerization. Flexible translucent sheets form as the polymer cools.

discoveries.name: Clay-bound organic recovery
discoveries.description: Dissolving the clay matrix to release trapped organics, then polymerizing the extract under heat. The clay layers preserved these molecules for billions of years.

discoveries.name: Regolith sintering test
discoveries.description: High-temperature fusion of crushed basalt with trace sulfur as binder. The pellets crack when they cool, but they hold.

discoveries.name: Sulfuric binder synthesis
discoveries.description: Thermal processing produces a sulfuric binder that cements basalt powder into solid concrete. Stronger than raw sintering.

discoveries.name: Hematite iron reduction
discoveries.description: Hydrogen reduction of hematite at 700°C strips the oxygen and leaves metallic iron powder. The reaction hisses and glows orange.

discoveries.name: Trace calcium extraction
discoveries.description: Dissolving calcium-bearing trace minerals into brine and concentrating the chloride salts. Useful electrolyte for internal systems.

discoveries.name: Aluminum silicate fiber
discoveries.description: High-temperature fusion of aluminum oxide with silica yields mullite fibers. Extreme heat resistance makes these ideal for insulation.

discoveries.name: Titanium wire draw
discoveries.description: Electrolytic reduction of dissolved titanium salts deposits metallic filaments. Lightweight and corrosion-resistant — the gold standard of structural wire.

discoveries.name: Iron wire drawing
discoveries.description: Electroplating concentrated iron traces onto a fine cathode. One atom-thick layer at a time, a usable filament emerges.

discoveries.name: Sulfur acid conversion
discoveries.description: Oxidizing concentrated trace sulfur to SO₃ and hydrating to sulfuric acid. Small batch, high purity.

discoveries.name: Magnesium flash powder
discoveries.description: Thermal reduction of concentrated magnesium salts to pure metal powder. Blindingly bright if ignited.

discoveries.name: Nickel-iron alloying
discoveries.description: Combining concentrated nickel with dissolved iron traces to precipitate a structural alloy. The meteorite's strength, refined by chemistry.
